[161:45]Saudi Arabia built a pipeline called the East-West Pipeline, and they're taking the oil in the Persian Gulf and

[161:52]they're piping it from east to west so that it can be exported in the Red Sea on their western coast.

[162:00]And so, for the first time since the war started, this pipeline is now at maximum volume.

[162:05]They're pumping 100% of that pipeline's capacity out the Red Sea, and this is going to prop

[162:12]up the oil market.

[162:14]So at least the global energy market is getting some of the Persian Gulf oil.

[162:20]A lot of it is stuck in the Persian Gulf just because that's where all the infrastructure

[162:24]is.

[162:25]The, the infrastructure was built to export all that oil through the Persian Gulf.

[162:32]But lately these countries have been hedging their bets because they've been anticipating

[162:35]this conflict by building some infrastructure to get some of the supply out the other way.

[162:42]Well, now that the who, the Houthis are targeting shipping in the Red Sea, what is that going

[162:47]to do?

[162:48]They say we're only targeting Israeli shipping, but that doesn't really matter.

[162:53]All the Houthis have to do is start launching drones and missiles.

[162:57]And what is going to happen is that insurance premiums

[163:01]that ensure the cargo on all ships that go through the Red Sea

[163:05]is going to skyrocket. It's going to be the same problem in the Persian Gulf.

[163:09]It will be cost prohibitive to ship

[163:13]through the Suez, Red Sea, and Bab El-Mandeb.

[163:17]And so all the commercial shipping that goes through the Red Sea

[163:23]And so, if commercial shipping was going to go through that shortcut, let's say from Europe

[163:28]to Asia, now it's going to have to go all the way around Africa.

[163:33]And it doesn't matter if the Houthis are only targeting Israeli ships.

[163:38]If projectiles are flying, if even one ship gets hit, insurance goes up, everybody is

[163:43]risk averse, costs go up, they're not going to go through the Red Sea.

[163:47]They're going to go the other way.

[163:49]And what this does is it increases transportation costs for everything.

[163:55]And it just makes common sense, it's a longer journey.

[163:58]It takes longer to ship across all of Africa than it does to go through the Red Sea.

[164:05]So it makes shipping on everything more expensive.

[164:13]Energy's becoming more expensive.

[164:16]Food is becoming more expensive.

[164:20]everything else gets more expensive

[164:22]because everything goes on a ship, a plane, a truck,

[164:26]all that runs on energy.
